text.title: ab-testing-basics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ab-testing-basics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Peeking: checking statistical significance before the pre-registered sample size is reached.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each look at results inflates the false-positive rate. At alpha=0.05, 5 peeks give ~20% Type I error instead of 5%. Use a sequential testing library or fix a single analysis date.

failure-mode.description: Multiple testing: running 5+ secondary metrics each at alpha=0.05 and reporting any p<0.05 as a win.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: With 5 independent tests the familywise error rate is 1-(0.95^5) ≈ 23%. Apply Bonferroni correction or report only confidence intervals for secondary metrics.

failure-mode.description: Changing experiment mid-run: modifying allocation, variants, or primary metric after users are already bucketed.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Users bucketed under the old allocation are not comparable to users bucketed under the new one. Results are confounded and uninterpretable.

failure-mode.description: Ignoring sample-ratio mismatch (SRM): the observed variant split deviates from the configured allocation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: SRM is evidence of a bucketing bug or logging failure. Analysis on an SRM dataset produces biased lift estimates. Always run chi-square SRM check before analysis.

failure-mode.description: Agent confuses relative vs absolute MDE: "+10% lift on 6% baseline" is +0.6pp absolute, not +10pp.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Forces units explicitly in every prompt and formula call. Wrong MDE input produces a wildly incorrect sample-size estimate.
